item.title: Some cool stuff
item.description:
Links

  • HIP docs: https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/
  • Khronos for HPC: https://www.khronos.org/sycl/
  • Hugh Perkins’ GPU: https://github.com/hughperkins/VeriGPU
  • NPU for LLMs: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.12962
  • Activation engine: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2512.23062
  • Accelerator for 1.58-bit LLM: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.15926 – 25 Tokens/s in 5W
  • VTR: https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing
  • ATMel: https://start.atmel.com/
  • HALO’s FRAME (opensource smartglasses): https://docs.brilliant.xyz/frame/frame/
  • FPGAs and DNNs: https://youtu.be/WWCWsub3YkE?si=vo1GEcfPHKgIRKZw
  • BORPH (FPGA-supportive OS): https://casper.berkeley.edu/wiki/BORPH
  • CASPERFPGA py lib: https://github.com/casper-astro/casperfpga

item.title: Rebellion via MDM Prison Break with Odin (Part I)
item.description:
This is the first part of my blog where I attempt to flash a new OS into a tablet, that has been carrying a firmware, locked down with heavy restrictions.

For context, I’ve got my hands on a government-provided Samsung tablet (won’t be disclosing model or the provider for paranoia-reasons) which has been “locked” for general usage, and allows only 8 applications that can be used (restricted using a third party MDM software), strictly for academic purposes (including instagram for whatever reasons?).

item.title: About Me
item.description:
I’m Lalith, and am curious about tech and social justice. This site mostly consists of my techie side, with a hint of other stuff as well in an (as you’ve already figured out) extremely informal tone.

I’ve tilted my career towards RTL Design cuz why not (ps. I like mathematics and physics). I’ve tasted a lil bit of everything I could (Linux, android application Reverse engineering, RTL Design, Computer Architecture, pure mathematics), and my list of ‘Stuff I love’ only grew.

item.title: Contact Me
item.description: You can contact me via Email or LinkedIn, can’t guarantee about insta DMs tho :)

item.title: Experience
item.description:
Working as an FPGA Dev, exploring various ways and applications of reconfigurable edge ML acceleration.
I’m also very passionate about Linux and computer architecture in general.

Here’s my Resume

item.description: Here are a few of my projects that I think were kinda fun!
